findings.description: run_with_retries resets `attempt = 0` every iteration so `while attempt < MAX_RETRIES` is always true; a job that keeps returning False (bad convert path, unknown kind) loops forever sleeping 2s each pass, pinning a worker thread at infinite cost and never advancing the queue.

Blocking time.sleep() (plus the blocking run_with_retries/subprocess.run it calls) inside the async poll_loop stalls the entire event loop, freezing every other coroutine in the web service; must use await asyncio.sleep and offload job execution to an executor.

Backoff never escalates: because `attempt` is 0 when the sleep is computed, RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS * (attempt + 1) is always 2.0s, defeating the intended exponential backoff (compounds the line-69 infinite-loop cost).

subprocess.run with capture_output=True buffers the child's entire stdout+stderr into memory unbounded (runaway convert balloons worker RAM), and the synchronous call blocks the event loop on the async path; the shell=True injection is real but defer detail to critic-security.
JobQueue.pop uses list.pop(0), which is O(n) per dequeue and O(n^2) to drain N jobs; use collections.deque.popleft() for O(1).
json.dumps(job.payload) (line 46) and sum(values) (line 56) are computed eagerly even when the info log level is disabled, a small wasted cost on hot echo/aggregate paths.
